rationale: G1's remaining round-2 ask is addressed. The malformed-once shim now has a real comparison operand: the first stdout payload is verbatim narrative containing exactly one schema-valid verdict object, and run 2 explicitly extracts that embedded object from the persisted .codex.log before canonical JSON comparison. The second payload is the same verdict as a clean standalone object, so the successful reserialize path can compare verdict, hard_block, and concrete_changes_required without relying on string equality. The shapes are consistent with the one-object acceptance rule because surrounding narrative makes the first payload rejected while still decodable for fidelity. I found no new unrunnable command in the 143 added lines.

findings.finding: The first payload now embeds one concrete verdict JSON object inside narrative text, and scenario run 2 instructs extracting that embedded object from .codex.log for canonical comparison against the final verdict file.

Step 3 rejects zero objects, multiple objects, or one object plus surrounding narrative. The malformed-once first payload intentionally falls into that rejection path, while the second payload is a clean standalone object for the reserialize success path.

findings.ask: No new unrunnable command in round-2 fix
findings.finding: I reviewed the added verification/manual-command text and ran diff checks plus plan validation. The new shell lines and manual invocations are runnable/documented in the same style as the surrounding plan; validate plan exits with only the pre-existing advisory decomposition warning.
